patches.find: about (ii) and (iii), where the dome's own refraction fix contradicts straight-line shadow-cone geometry; it deliberately is not deployed against (i).
patches.replace:
about (ii) and (iii), where the dome's own refraction fix contradicts straight-line shadow-cone geometry; it deliberately is not deployed against (i).

The scaling formula itself consumes shadow geometry, not magnitudes. The dome's own source code defines the per-station scaling factor (FSF) in check_eclipse_fsf.py (dome GitHub repository) as fsf = (h / r) / (h_b / r_bou), where h = H(r) is the dome height at the station's dome-coordinate radius and r_bou is Boulder's dome-coordinate radius. The inputs are pure geometry: station radius and the dome's H(r) profile. Apparent eclipse magnitude is not an input to FSF; it enters the outer prediction equation as the separate coverage factor (ΔB = baseline × coverage × FSF, monitor.py Domain 10). A defender who rescues single-station predictions by pointing to observationally-fixed eclipse magnitude cannot repeat that move for the per-station scaling table: FSF is computed from dome-coordinate shadow geometry by construction, and under the dome's own n(r) medium that shadow geometry is bent, not a straight-line chord.
patches.rationale: ISS-1009: Insert direct FSF code citation from check_eclipse_fsf.py after the paragraph asserting scaling formula consumes shadow geometry. EXP-165 confirmed formula is fsf=(h/r)/(h_b/r_bou) — purely geometric, no eclipse magnitude input. Closes ISS-1009.

patches.find: this WIN confirms nothing beyond internal algebraic consistency.
patches.replace:
this WIN confirms nothing beyond internal algebraic consistency.

Direct dome source: check_eclipse_fsf.py defines FSF as (H(r)/r) / (H(r_BOU)/r_BOU) — purely geometric. Station-specific amplitudes are produced by plugging dome-coordinate station radii and the dome H(r) profile into this ratio. The formula does not take eclipse magnitude as input; coverage is applied separately as ΔB = baseline × coverage × FSF in monitor.py Domain 10 (line 515). The published E001–E009 FSF values (EBR 1.029, HAD 1.471, LER 2.075, …) reproduce to within ~3% under the V51 ellipse H(r) with V51 station radii (check_fsf.py), but do NOT reproduce under the V50.6 exponential H(r) with V50.6 radii (HAD computes to 1.89 vs published 1.47). The baseline station radii move between dome versions: BOU shifts from r=5715 (V50.6) to r=9983 (V51). The 'derivation from V12 geometry' claim is therefore version-unstable.
patches.rationale: ISS-1009 / EXP-165: Append verbatim FSF formula citation to WIN-044 detail_evidence. The formula (H(r)/r)/(H(r_BOU)/r_BOU) from check_eclipse_fsf.py confirms no eclipse magnitude input. Published E001-E009 values reproduce only under V51 ellipse, not V50.6 exponential — version instability admission.
